detection_rate.long_name: Measles virus detection rate in wastewater surveillance
detection_rate.short_description: Percentage of wastewater samples with measles virus detection
detection_rate.long_description: The detection rate represents the proportion of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across participating wastewater treatment facilities. This metric is calculated by dividing the total number of detections by the total number of samples collected and multiplying by 100. Higher detection rates may indicate increased measles virus circulation in the community served by the wastewater treatment facilities.
detection_rate.statement: In {location}, {value}% of wastewater samples tested positive for measles virus

detection_count.long_name: Total number of measles virus detections in wastewater
detection_count.short_description: Count of wastewater samples with measles virus detected
detection_count.long_description: The total number of wastewater samples that tested positive for measles virus RNA across all participating wastewater treatment facilities in the geographic area. This represents the raw count of detections without adjustment for the number of samples collected or population served.
detection_count.statement: In {location}, measles virus was detected in {value} wastewater samples

detection_flag.long_name: Binary indicator for measles virus detection in wastewater
detection_flag.short_description: Binary flag indicating whether measles was detected (1) or not detected (0)
detection_flag.long_description: A binary indicator that equals 1 if any wastewater sample tested positive for measles virus RNA in the geographic area and time period, and 0 if no samples tested positive. This provides a simple yes/no indicator of measles presence in wastewater, regardless of the number of detections or samples collected.
detection_flag.statement: In {location}, measles virus {value, select, 1{was detected} 0{was not detected}} in wastewater

sample_count.long_name: Total number of wastewater samples tested for measles
sample_count.short_description: Count of wastewater samples tested for measles virus
sample_count.long_description: The total number of wastewater samples collected and tested for measles virus RNA across all participating wastewater treatment facilities in the geographic area. This represents the denominator used to calculate detection rates.
sample_count.statement: In {location}, {value} wastewater samples were tested for measles virus

sewershed_count.long_name: Number of wastewater treatment facility sewersheds reporting data
sewershed_count.short_description: Count of sewersheds participating in measles surveillance
sewershed_count.long_description: The number of unique wastewater treatment facility sewersheds that submitted measles virus testing data for the geographic area and time period. A sewershed is the geographic area served by a wastewater treatment facility.
sewershed_count.statement: In {location}, {value} sewersheds reported measles wastewater surveillance data

population_served.long_name: Total population served by wastewater treatment facilities reporting measles data
population_served.short_description: Number of people served by sewersheds reporting measles surveillance data
population_served.long_description: The total population served by wastewater treatment facilities that submitted measles virus testing data. This represents the coverage of the wastewater surveillance system and indicates the number of people whose wastewater is being monitored for measles virus.
population_served.statement: In {location}, wastewater surveillance for measles covers a population of {value} people

sewershed_id.long_name: Unique identifier for wastewater treatment facility sewershed
sewershed_id.short_description: Unique ID for each wastewater treatment facility sewershed
sewershed_id.long_description: A unique identifier assigned to each wastewater treatment facility sewershed participating in measles surveillance. This field is only present in the county-level (site-level) data file and allows tracking of individual sewershed reporting over time. A sewershed represents the geographic catchment area served by a specific wastewater treatment facility.

detection_status.long_name: Categorical detection status for measles in wastewater
detection_status.short_description: Detection status: Detection, No Detection, or No Data
detection_status.long_description: A categorical variable indicating the measles virus detection status at a wastewater treatment facility. Values include 'Detection' (measles RNA detected in samples), 'No Detection' (samples tested but no measles RNA found), or 'No Data' (no samples available or tested). This field is only present in the county-level (site-level) data file and provides qualitative detection information for individual sewersheds.
